title: Employee Transfer

jokes.text: Hey, sport! I heard someone got engaged, you dog! Yeah. God! Nothing can hurt you now. You're a man in love!
jokes.punchline: Jim's flat, unenthusiastic 'Yeah' response
jokes.explanation: Standard ironic contrast between Michael's over-the-top enthusiasm and Jim's deflated reality

jokes.text: I was there, and that dude is not engaged. I'm not a big believer in therapy, but I would go into my own pocket to cover his copay.
jokes.punchline: Offering to pay for their therapy
jokes.explanation: Sharp observation with unexpected escalation to paying for therapy - good economy and structure

jokes.text: Big idea, double wedding! Me, Angela, you, Holly. No. We would never do that. And if we did, it would be with Jim and Pam.
jokes.punchline: Jim immediately shutting it down, then clarifying they'd choose Jim and Pam instead
jokes.explanation: Classic Michael presumption getting shut down, with added insult of preferring Jim and Pam

jokes.text: Michael, I got my bridesmaid's dress. Wow, so quickly. Yeah, and you said I could get it in any color I wanted, so I picked white.
jokes.setup: Getting bridesmaid dress quickly in chosen color
jokes.punchline: Choosing white (traditionally bride's color)
jokes.explanation: Perfect Angela move - passive-aggressive and inappropriate, very character-specific

jokes.text: Hello? Mom, I'm getting married. No, you're not. Why do you always do that? Whenever I'm getting married, you don't believe me.
jokes.setup: Michael calling mom to announce engagement
jokes.punchline: Mom immediately not believing him, and his complaint about 'whenever I'm getting married'
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character work - implies Michael has fake-announced engagements multiple times before

jokes.text: Are you getting married? No.
jokes.setup: Direct question after the engagement announcement
jokes.punchline: Flat 'No' admission
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective deflation - economy of words makes it work

jokes.text: Psych.
jokes.punchline: Trying to play it off as a joke with 'Psych'
jokes.explanation: Pathetic attempt to save face - very Michael but not particularly clever

jokes.text: So I've returned my bridesmaid dress and it was on sale, so I'm out a hundred dollars.
jokes.punchline: Focusing on personal financial loss rather than embarrassment
jokes.explanation: Perfect Angela priority - money over emotions, good callback to earlier dress joke

jokes.text: And I'm out a fiancée.
jokes.punchline: Michael's parallel complaint about losing fake fiancée
jokes.explanation: Good parallel structure with Angela's line, absurd since he never had a real fiancée

jokes.text: It's the receipt to my bridesmaid dress. What's that doing there? I'll take care of that for you.
jokes.punchline: Bridesmaid dress receipt mixed in with customer surveys
jokes.explanation: Minor visual callback but not particularly strong on its own

jokes.text: Kelly Kapoor is our dusky, exotic, customer service rep.
jokes.punchline: Inappropriate racial description
jokes.explanation: Typical Michael inappropriate workplace description - cringeworthy but predictable

jokes.text: Sort of a 'Kapoor's List.' Schindler's List parody.
jokes.punchline: Inappropriate Holocaust reference pun
jokes.explanation: Classically inappropriate Michael reference - character-perfect but offensive

jokes.text: That's not appropriate. No.
jokes.punchline: Immediately recognizing it's inappropriate
jokes.explanation: Michael's rare moment of self-awareness makes the original joke worse/better

jokes.text: but Pam's a gold digger. Hey, New York ain't free.
jokes.setup: Jim calling Pam a gold digger
jokes.punchline: Pam's matter-of-fact justification
jokes.explanation: Good relationship dynamic - Pam playing along with Jim's joke

jokes.text: It's the world's tiniest Bluetooth. I found it in a Japanese gadget store in the Village.
jokes.punchline: Absurdly specific location detail
jokes.explanation: Sets up the Bluetooth gag that runs through the episode - functional setup

jokes.text: We wanted to stay on the phone all day, but the company has a policy against eight-hour personal calls, so we're not telling anyone.
jokes.setup: Wanting to stay connected all day
jokes.punchline: Obvious policy violation being treated as sneaky
jokes.explanation: Self-aware joke about obvious policy violation while actively violating it

jokes.text: Pam, what do you want on your coffee? Sprinkle of cinnamon. Sprinkle of cinnamon.
jokes.punchline: Jim repeating Pam's coffee order aloud
jokes.explanation: Sweet relationship moment that also shows how the Bluetooth works

jokes.text: Dwight, your feedback is horrible. That's impossible.
jokes.setup: Delivering bad news about customer feedback
jokes.punchline: Dwight's immediate denial of possibility
jokes.explanation: Classic Dwight inability to accept criticism - very character-consistent

jokes.text: I'm getting that queasy feeling that sometimes accompanies jokes. Do I look like I am joking?
jokes.setup: Wondering if bad feedback is a joke
jokes.punchline: Dwight's clinical description of feeling queasy from jokes
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Dwight-specific way to describe suspicion - clinical and weird

jokes.text: Impossible to say, I can't see myself. You're not.
jokes.setup: Asked if he looks like he's laughing
jokes.punchline: Literal interpretation of not being able to see himself
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dwight logic - overly literal interpretation of simple question

jokes.text: Yo, Tommy Tuna. Did you get your scores yet? No. I got mine, they were really good.
jokes.punchline: Bragging about his good scores to someone with bad ones
jokes.explanation: Typical Andy obliviousness and self-promotion - character-consistent

jokes.text: I miss him.
jokes.punchline: Random emotional outburst about missing someone
jokes.explanation: Andy's emotional instability showing through - brief but effective

jokes.text: That's my mug. Sorry, I was just... It was right here. Right. Well, it's mine, so if you could pour it out and get another one.
jokes.punchline: Andy's unreasonable demand to pour out coffee and get new mug
jokes.explanation: Building tension for the mug reveal - Andy being unreasonably territorial

jokes.text: That is my mug, so give it back. How can you even be sure? That's my face on it.
jokes.punchline: Revelation that Andy's face is literally on the mug
jokes.explanation: Good visual reveal that explains the territorial behavior

jokes.text: Make the face. I don't see it. Dude, that is my face.
jokes.setup: Proving identity through face on mug
jokes.punchline: Jim not seeing resemblance, Andy's frustration
jokes.explanation: Physical comedy of face-making combined with Jim's deadpan denial

jokes.text: What color mustard is his shirt, yellow or Dijon? It is spicy brown, actually.
jokes.setup: Bluetooth conversation about Dwight's shirt color
jokes.punchline: Overly specific mustard variety description
jokes.explanation: Clever extension of mustard metaphor with unexpected variety

jokes.text: I caught Jim talking to himself several times today. What a loser. Get a friend, loser!
jokes.punchline: Dwight's cruel mockery of someone for not having friends
jokes.explanation: Ironic since Dwight himself has no friends, and he's wrong about Jim

jokes.text: I found the best 'tentist' on the East Coast.
jokes.punchline: Made-up word 'tentist' like dentist
jokes.explanation: Michael's tendency to make up professional-sounding words

jokes.text: He personally tented Giuliani's first and third weddings.
jokes.punchline: Notably skipping Giuliani's second wedding
jokes.explanation: Subtle implication about Giuliani's marriage troubles - smart reference

jokes.text: Jim, they are poopy.
jokes.punchline: Using childish word 'poopy' for serious feedback
jokes.explanation: Michael's childish vocabulary for adult problems

jokes.text: Jim Halpert is smudge and arrogant. I think he means smug. Arrogance. Michael, I'm just trying to... And there is our smudgeness.
jokes.punchline: Michael doubling down on wrong word despite correction
jokes.explanation: Classic Michael malapropism with stubborn doubling down - very quotable

jokes.text: I need a decent bonus because I'm actually in the process of buying my parents' house so that they can retire.
jokes.setup: Explaining need for good customer scores
jokes.punchline: Revealing sweet gesture of helping parents retire
jokes.explanation: More character development than comedy - shows Jim's maturation

jokes.text: And if history tells us anything, it's that you can't go wrong buying a house you can't afford.
jokes.punchline: Reference to 2008 housing crisis with ironic confidence
jokes.explanation: Excellent topical reference to financial crisis - smart and timely

jokes.text: Pam doesn't know about the house, so it's a fun surprise.
jokes.punchline: Calling potential financial disaster a 'fun surprise'
jokes.explanation: Jim's optimistic spin on potentially problematic secret

jokes.text: Maybe it's 'cause you spent the whole year flirting with the receptionist. Little bit. Worth it.
jokes.punchline: Jim admitting it was worth the bad scores
jokes.explanation: Sweet callback to Jim/Pam romance being worth professional consequences

jokes.text: Jim, what is that called? Micro-gement. Boom. Yes.
jokes.punchline: Jim making up 'micro-gement' portmanteau
jokes.explanation: Jim playing into Michael's need for business terminology

jokes.text: Ring, ring. Hello? Hello, this is Dwight Schrute from the Dunder Mifflin paper company.
jokes.punchline: Dwight taking fake phone call completely seriously
jokes.explanation: Sets up the Bill Buttlicker sequence - functional setup

jokes.text: I am Bill Buttlicker.
jokes.setup: Jim giving fake name as customer
jokes.punchline: Ridiculous surname 'Buttlicker'
jokes.explanation: Iconic absurd name that becomes legendary - simple but perfect

jokes.text: Really? That's your real name? How dare you? My family built this country, by the way.
jokes.punchline: Jim taking offense and claiming family legacy
jokes.explanation: Escalating the absurdity while making Dwight the villain

jokes.text: No. I'm just on the phone with this stupid salesman. He's so dumb. Probably just going to keep him on the line forever and not buy anything.
jokes.setup: Jim pretending to take another call
jokes.punchline: Insulting Dwight while he listens on hold
jokes.explanation: Brilliant cruelty while maintaining character - Dwight's torture continues

jokes.text: Sorry, you just have to speak a little bit louder, I'm hard of hearing. He's hard of... He's an old man.
jokes.punchline: Michael's helpful stage direction
jokes.explanation: Building to the shouting climax, Michael's helpful narration

jokes.text: Buttlicker, our prices have never been lower! Stop it! Stop it!
jokes.setup: Dwight forced to shout at customer
jokes.punchline: Michael stopping him after he finally yells the name
jokes.explanation: Perfect payoff - Dwight forced to shout absurd name, Michael's horror

jokes.text: The three words I would describe you as is aggressive, hostile and definitely difficult.
jokes.punchline: Using exact words from original customer complaints
jokes.explanation: Good callback to the original complaint words - proves the point

jokes.text: I'm going to buy $1,000,000 worth of paper products today. See how it's done? You are the master.
jokes.punchline: Impossible million-dollar sale, Michael's pride
jokes.explanation: Michael's delusional pride in fake sale sets up the final twist

jokes.text: You have to fire the salesman that treated me so terribly. Don't do it, Michael. It's a million-dollar sale.
jokes.punchline: Dwight begging not to be fired, Michael's dilemma
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation of the prank - puts Michael in impossible position

jokes.text: It's two stories, heated, and it has a bridal suite for my bridal sweet.
jokes.punchline: Pun on 'suite/sweet' for bride
jokes.explanation: Predictable pun but fits Michael's character

jokes.text: I don't want to be married in a tent like a hobo. Hobos live in trains.
jokes.punchline: Dwight's pedantic correction about hobo housing
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dwight correction - technically accurate but misses the point

jokes.text: Done and done-er.
jokes.punchline: Made-up escalation of 'done and done'
jokes.explanation: Michael's tendency to escalate common phrases

jokes.text: There has to be a barn that's old enough that you can see the stars through the roof slats, when you lay on your back. And antique tools to look at when you roll over.
jokes.punchline: Overly romantic and impractical barn specifications
jokes.explanation: Angela's impractical romanticism about rustic venue

jokes.text: They might be listening to us. What's that? Who is 'they'? Customer service might be monitoring this conversation! In this car?
jokes.punchline: Thinking customer service monitors car conversations
jokes.explanation: Classic Dwight paranoia escalating to absurd levels

jokes.text: Who stands to benefit from our downfall? The mob? Maybe NASA. Could be the mob.
jokes.punchline: Random organizations like NASA as suspects
jokes.explanation: Dwight's conspiracy theories involving completely unrelated organizations

jokes.text: Cute shoes online. How many shoes do you need? I don't know, two, maybe three, if one wears out.
jokes.setup: Evidence of money laundering through shoe purchases
jokes.punchline: Jim's practical male perspective on shoe quantity
jokes.explanation: Standard gender difference joke about shoe collecting

jokes.text: How many shoes do you need? I'm not talking to you. Who are you talking to? Pam. She's not here, Jim. No, she's not.
jokes.setup: Jim appearing to talk to no one
jokes.punchline: Dwight pointing out Pam isn't there, Jim's agreement
jokes.explanation: Pays off the Bluetooth setup with Dwight's confusion

jokes.text: I found it. I found the perfect place. A local bed and breakfast which is on a 60-acre beet farm, and even better, I have an in with the owner.
jokes.punchline: It's his own beet farm
jokes.explanation: Classic Dwight self-promotion disguised as helpfulness

jokes.text: Well, stop. Hold your breath. I still hear it. Who's there? Kelly, is that you?
jokes.punchline: Demanding Kelly stop breathing, then hearing her anyway
jokes.explanation: Building tension toward Kelly revelation but not particularly strong

jokes.text: You always say that, and I almost never know. What are you up to, girl? Did Phyllis put you up to this? Stanley? Are they paying you?
jokes.punchline: Kelly's honest confusion about Dwight's usual accusations
jokes.explanation: Kelly's meta-observation about Dwight's constant accusations

jokes.text: You just can't come into my nook and call me stupid. And maybe if you were a little bit more nice and polite, then people wouldn't give you such bad customer reviews.
jokes.punchline: Pointing out the obvious reason for bad reviews
jokes.explanation: Kelly stating the obvious truth Dwight can't see

jokes.text: That's what she said. That's what she said! That's what she said! Good one.
jokes.setup: Kelly saying 'get out of my nook'
jokes.punchline: Multiple 'that's what she said' responses
jokes.explanation: Overused running gag - predictable and not particularly clever

jokes.text: Have you ever had a conversation with Kelly where she didn't go on for 15 minutes without taking a breath? No, actually.
jokes.punchline: Pam confirming Kelly always talks nonstop normally
jokes.explanation: Sharp observation about Kelly's character trait being notably absent

jokes.text: Her America's Got Talent finale party over the summer. That's crazy. It was packed. I thought everyone was there. You were there. I remember you being there. I wasn't, but thank you.
jokes.punchline: Ryan insisting Jim was there when he wasn't
jokes.explanation: Social awkwardness of insisting someone was somewhere they weren't

jokes.text: Boom! Kelly the whole time. Let's get her. No, no, no, no. Dwight, Dwight, Dwight.
jokes.setup: Dwight excited about solving the mystery
jokes.punchline: Jim trying to restrain Dwight's aggressive enthusiasm
jokes.explanation: Dwight's violent enthusiasm being restrained by reasonable Jim

jokes.text: Let me paint you a picture of a little girl from southern India, who despite being welcomed into this country will now spend the rest of her life in prison for a crime she did commit.
jokes.punchline: 'Crime she did commit' and life in prison for falsifying surveys
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Dwight overstatement - dramatic immigrant story for minor office infraction

jokes.text: I was raped. You cannot say 'I was raped' and expect all your problems to go away, Kelly. Not again. Don't keep doing that.
jokes.setup: Kelly trying to deflect with serious claim
jokes.punchline: Michael's exasperation about her using this excuse repeatedly
jokes.explanation: Dark callback revealing Kelly's pattern of false serious claims

jokes.text: I did it because you guys didn't come to my party, and you said you would try to, and then you didn't even show up, and so you're bad friends.
jokes.punchline: Childish motivation of hurt feelings about party attendance
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kelly motivation - petty social slight leads to professional sabotage

jokes.text: I have an enormous amount of trouble trying to get people to come to my place. And I hate it. I can't tell you how much leftover guacamole I have ended up eating over the years.
jokes.punchline: Specific detail about eating leftover guacamole alone
jokes.explanation: Genuinely touching and specific detail that humanizes Kelly's actions

jokes.text: I don't even know why I make it in such great quantities.
jokes.punchline: Not understanding why she makes so much for empty parties
jokes.explanation: Good follow-up that shows Kelly's hopeful delusion about party attendance

jokes.text: There's free wine and cheese at the Chuck Close retrospective. Let's go. That's going to be great. Who's Chuck Close? I love Chuck Close and his photorealist paintings.
jokes.setup: Alex inviting Pam to art event
jokes.punchline: Pam asking who Chuck Close is then immediately claiming to love him
jokes.explanation: Pam's attempt to sound cultured immediately undermined by ignorance

jokes.text: Why did you come to New York in the first place? Because they have a great design program, and I wanted to see if I was any good at it. And I wanted to work on my art, too.
jokes.punchline: Pam's honest self-reflection about artistic goals
jokes.explanation: More dramatic than comedic - character development moment

jokes.text: Is that the Matsahashi B-400? The world's tiniest Bluetooth. May I? Don't.
jokes.punchline: Jim's immediate protective 'Don't'
jokes.explanation: Callback to earlier Bluetooth gag with Jim's protective instinct

jokes.text: I will work tirelessly for you over the coming months and be at your constant disposal. Please feel free to call or stop by any time of the day or night.
jokes.punchline: Offering 24/7 availability for wedding planning
jokes.explanation: Classic Dwight over-commitment and lack of boundaries

jokes.text: This wedding is officially out of your hands. Thank the good Lord. Deal! Okay. What are we talking pricewise? You already said, 'Deal.' Pay him whatever he wants.
jokes.punchline: Michael's relief leading to agreeing before hearing price
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Michael's desperation leading to poor negotiation

tmdb_overview: Pam is embarrassed when she is the only person at corporate wearing a costume on Halloween.
